rulings.Case Title: SARA R. JULIAN IRREVOCABLE TRUST CREATED FOR THE BENEFIT OF ISAAC JULIAN BOOTS,DATED 1/16/18 EST. PURSUANT TO COURT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. No courtesy copy of the proof of service. No original bank statements. Paragraph 4 of the petition refers to "the SNT". Paragraph 8 refers to a payment to the trustee, but the accounting seems to reflect that that payment was reimbursed to the trust (?). There is no payee identified for the investment expenses on p. 12 of the accounting. There are a greater number of shares of the four funds held in account xxx1749, but no informational schedule showing any reinvestments. The attorney costs reflected on invoice 35706 for messenger fees on 7/2/19 predate this accounting period and do not appear to be for this trust (there was no second declaration filed on or around 7/2/19). Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/22/22. Time entries will be reviewed with the related SNT. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEOLA J. WASHINGTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The executor requests an additional 12 months to administer the estate, but this is not in the petition caption. [CRC 7.102.] The executor is also the executor of the post-deceased brother's will. Who are his beneficiaries? They must be identified and they must be given notice. Until a personal representative is appointed for the other post-deceased beneficiary, the accounting cannot be approved. The disbursements do not appear to show any payment of the probate referee's commissions, as alleged in paragraph 37 of the petition. Are the payments for the Louisiana properties just for this decedent's proportional interest in such properties? Copies of the bills should be filed to confirm this. Why are the payments for insurance so varied until 10/1/20? To whom was the $1,620.00 check written prior to death made out? The Bank of the West statements show "POA", not the estate... Why has the executor not sold volatile securities and tangible personal property yet? Is the FMV of the automobile really still the same as on the date of death? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/22/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOYCE M. JOSEPH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Disposition Of The Reserve
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/3/22. The caption of the pleading filed on 6/21/22 did not fully disclose the nature of the petition, which appears to be a petition for additional time for estate administration and an accounting. [LRSF 14.15 B.] No filing fee was paid as a result of the incomplete pleading caption, and, therefore, no hearing date was set. As of 7/5/22, no courtesy copy of the pleading, no proof of service, no bank statement(s), and no proposed order have been received by the assigned examiner. A new, properly titled PETITION must be filed and a filing fee must be paid in order to obtain a HEARING DATE for the accounting petition. A courtesy copy of the petition, proof of service, and the original bank statement(s), accompanied by the attorney's or fiduciary's statement about retention of the original statement(s), must be delivered to Room 202, along with the proposed order, promptly upon obtaining a hearing date. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSEANNE BERTUCELLI AS TRUSTEE OF THE I TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Relief For Breach Of Trust; Breach Of Trust Trustee De Son Tort/De Facto Trustee; Complaint For Fraudulent Concealment **Filing Date Backdated To 4/1/21 Per Ex Parte Order Filed 5/19/21**
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copy of the declaration filed on 3/11/22 has been received yet by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] (Multiple copies of the second amended petition have been received, but not the declaration.) The proof of service filed on 6/9/22 still does not show the full caption of the pleading on the face of the Notice of Hearing. (A proposed order is not filed and should not be part of the caption of the "petition, application, report, or account" identified on the face of a Notice of Hearing.) The service list attached to the proof of service still shows notice mailed to most of the interested parties in care of an attorney "per written request", but no such written request appears to have been filed yet. The addresses of the interested parties were set forth in the petition, to which addresses notice should have been mailed to those parties. Courtesy copies of the declaration filed on 3/11/22 and any other papers filed to address the issues noted in the hearing notes for the 3/14/22 hearing must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/22/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JONATHAN NGUYEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the courtesy copy of the proof of service filed on 6/27/22 was received by the examiner on 7/6/22, not 7/1/22, the Acknowledgement of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is missing the phone number in item 2, and the petition never clearly set forth how the petitioner, a "nephew", is related to the decedent, as required by LRSF 14.18 (he is presumably the son of one of the surviving sisters, not of any predeceased sibling). Status of filing the I&A is set for 11/14/2022. No appearance will be required on 11/14/2022 if the I&A is filed at least one week before 11/14/2022. Status of closing the estate is set for 7/24/2023. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 7/24/2023. Appearance will be required on 7/24/2023 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NG MOY LUK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The petition and supplement allege Decedent died July 4, 2012. The alleged certified copy of the order for probate from Hong Kong alleges Decedent died July 24, 2012. 2) Court to determine whether to require more efforts to locate Poon Ko Sing and Poon Woon Tai, as the only efforts stated in the Declaration were to contact family members, or to waive notice.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE SARANTITIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) Counsel has not filed a response to the Examiner note about local rule 14.16 F instead a Second Amended petition was filed that does not address this issue and still requests waiver of bond. a. LRSF 14.16 F: A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. b. LRSF 14.16 L: The bond amount will be rounded up to the nearest thousand dollars of the estimated value of the property to be protected.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALFRED JOSHUA DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) "Authorization to Administer Under the Independent Administration of Estates Act" box must be checked in the petition caption. 2) Item 3d(2)(b) of the petition must specify annual gross income from personal property. 3) Some heirs identified at item 8 have not waived the petitioner's bond. If the petitioner is unable to obtain bond waivers from all other heirs, please state whether the petitioner is bondable for $8,000,000. 4) A review of the competing petition filed by Jerry Smith indicates the existence of additional heirs not identified at item 8 (issue of Adelene & George McCutcheon). Jerry Smith's address must also be stated. 5) A proof of service attached to the DE-121 form filed on 06/14/2022 does not show notice was given to Jerry Smith and issue of Adelene & George McCutcheon. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALFRED JOSHUA DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Gloria Thomas, as the decedent's sister, has priority for appointment over Jerry Smith, the decedent's nephew. Jerry Smith alleges that Gloria Thomas is not bondable for $8,000,000 to qualify as administrator. Gloria Thomas requests that her bond be waived, but some beneficiaries have not waived her bond. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA HOLLANDER LYON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/29/22. (No earlier available date.) Item 8 of the petition is missing the decedent's husband and his date of death. [LRSF 14.18 C.] No declination by the other named co-executor, Jonathan Victor Lyon, has been filed. The names of the grandchildren listed in item 8 of the petition are not consistent with the names in the will. No explanation is given. A supplement to the petition must be filed, identifying the decedent's husband, who was named in her will, and setting forth his date of death, clearly stating that the grandchildren listed in item 8 are the grandchildren named in the will, and attaching the declination by the other nominated co-executor. A courtesy copy of the supplement must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 8/19/22 to avoid a continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
